title: romulea columnae var. subalbida
intro: Romulea columnae var. subalbida is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the Romulea or Corn Lily. It is a perennial plant with white or pale yellow flowers and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions or decoctions, and it may be used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and digestive issues.
